issues.label: Generic Hero Language
issues.description: Hero section communicates value but lacks specificity about what outcome the visitor will actually get. Common patterns: 'We deliver beautiful design', 'Expert design solutions', 'Design that works' — these don't answer 'Why should I care right now?'
common_triggers: Headline written for internal team, not the customer
Copying competitor language without differentiation
Focusing on what you do rather than what the customer gains
Using industry jargon that feels impressive internally but confuses visitors

issues.label: Soft CTA Copy
issues.description: CTA button present but divorced from a clear next step or outcome. Common: 'Get Started', 'Learn More', 'Contact Us' without context of what happens next or what problem gets solved by clicking.
common_triggers: CTA copied from template without customisation
Fear of being too direct or salesy
No clear next step defined in the sales process
Multiple competing CTAs with no primary action

issues.label: CTA Lacks Outcome Bridge
issues.description: Asks visitor to take an action (sign up, book call, submit info) without explaining what they'll get from it. No bridge between problem → action → result. Feels transactional rather than beneficial.
common_triggers: CTA designed around the seller's goal (lead capture) not the buyer's desire
No micro-copy explaining what happens after the click
Mismatch between preceding content and the CTA ask
Generic button labels used across multiple page sections

issues.label: Features Before Outcomes
issues.description: Page structure prioritizes how you work (our process, our team, our methodology) before establishing why the visitor should care. Delays addressing visitor's actual problem or desired outcome.
common_triggers: Content written by the product or ops team, not a marketer
Pride in process overriding customer empathy
No customer journey mapping done before writing copy
Following a template that leads with 'about us'

issues.label: Offer Unclear at First Glance
issues.description: Within 10 seconds, the visitor cannot clearly articulate what the company does or what they'll get by working together. Confusion between service type, outcomes, and offering model.
common_triggers: Multiple service lines presented without a primary offer
Positioning written for existing clients who already know the business
Overly clever or abstract brand language
No clarity on delivery model (subscription, project, retainer)

issues.label: Weak Above-the-Fold
issues.description: The section that appears without scrolling doesn't establish a compelling reason to scroll further. Missing or weak: headline clarity, urgent signal, differentiation, reason to engage.
common_triggers: Hero image chosen for aesthetics over communication
Headline and subheadline don't work together to build the argument
No visual or copy hook creating curiosity or urgency
Navigation taking up too much above-fold real estate

issues.label: Visual Clutter & Competing Elements
issues.description: Homepage or key page shows too many features, sections, calls to action, or visual styles. Visitor attention fragments. No clear focal point. Everything feels equally important.
common_triggers: Feature requests from multiple stakeholders added without curation
No design system or visual hierarchy rules in place
Every business goal given equal visual weight
Iterative additions over time without a holistic review

issues.label: Cluttered Page Layout
issues.description: Visual hierarchy is weak. Text, images, whitespace don't guide eye flow. Sections feel disconnected. Page feels crowded even if there's technically enough whitespace. Navigation of page is confusing.
common_triggers: Content-first approach without layout planning
Too many font sizes, weights, or colours in use
Section padding and margins inconsistent across the page
Images and text competing at the same visual weight

issues.label: Trust Signals Hidden or Absent
issues.description: Logos, testimonials, certifications, case studies, or social proof are absent or placed in low-visibility areas (footer, buried below fold). Visitor has no easy way to build confidence in credibility.
common_triggers: Testimonials added as an afterthought in the footer
Client logos buried below the fold or on a separate page
Fear of asking clients for testimonials leading to zero social proof
Case study results not quantified or made specific

issues.label: No Clear Visual Reading Priority
issues.description: Font sizes, colors, spacing, and contrast don't create a clear priority for what visitor should read first, second, third. Everything competes equally. Eye has nowhere natural to land.
common_triggers: Design decisions made by feel rather than hierarchy rules
No defined H1/H2/H3/body type scale in the design system
Colour used decoratively rather than to signal importance
Multiple designers contributing without shared guidelines

issues.label: Poor Mobile Experience
issues.description: On mobile devices: text too small, buttons too hard to tap, layout breaks, images don't scale, CTAs buried. Navigation or critical content is difficult to access without zooming or horizontal scrolling.
common_triggers: Site designed on desktop and not properly tested on mobile
Fixed-width containers not using responsive units
Tap targets smaller than 44x44px minimum
Hero images cropped or hidden on mobile, removing key context

issues.label: Too Much Mental Effort Required
issues.description: Visitor needs to synthesize too many concepts, read long paragraphs, or connect dots to understand value. Jargon or complex explanations without simplification. Decision fatigue sets in quickly.
common_triggers: Subject matter experts writing copy without simplification
Multiple service tiers or options presented simultaneously
Long paragraphs where bullet points would work better
Industry jargon assumed to be universally understood

issues.label: No Social Proof Present
issues.description: Zero third-party validation. No customer names, quotes, case study results, client logos, or outcome numbers. Visitor has no evidence that you deliver what you claim.
common_triggers: Newly launched site without client history
NDA restrictions preventing named case studies
No systematic process for collecting testimonials
Relying on portfolio work without outcome data

issues.label: Speaks to No One Specifically
issues.description: Messaging is so broad it could apply to any business. No indication that this company understands a specific customer type's problems. Visitor doesn't feel seen or understood.
common_triggers: Fear of niching down and excluding potential customers
Multiple ICPs being served without dedicated pages
Positioning written around capabilities rather than customer problems
No customer persona work done before writing copy

issues.label: Confusing Navigation Structure
issues.description: Visitor unclear on next step or path to take action. Menu structure confusing, next button placement unclear, or multiple competing paths create decision paralysis. Unclear how to progress.
common_triggers: Navigation menu organised around internal teams, not user needs
Mega-menus with too many options causing choice paralysis
No sticky nav or fixed CTA visible on scroll
Back button dependency with no clear forward flow through the site

issues.label: Too Many Steps Before Action
issues.description: Visitor must read extensively or scroll through many sections before they encounter an actionable CTA. By then, attention has dropped. No early low-friction entry point to engage.
common_triggers: Page structured as an essay rather than a decision flow
First CTA placed after four or more sections of content
No micro-commitments or intermediate engagement steps
Long form pages without anchor CTAs on each section
